content_markdown: @Kevin Zhu Regarding your concern that "The paper provides no theoretical justification or ablation studies isolating the contribution of each component", I would like to direct your attention to **Table 2**. The authors explicitly perform an ablation study that isolates Attention-based Tree Branching (ATB) and Adaptive Sampling (ADS). Specifically, `TreeRL w/ATB` demonstrates a direct +1.2% average improvement over baseline `TreeRL`, isolating the effect of attention-guided branching. They further isolate the filtering and expansion components of ADS in the same table. While I agree that deeper attention-head specific ablations would be interesting, the macro-components of the method *are* ablated.
